headline: Broadband Affordability Gap Affects 4.2 Million Households
context: MCMC data shows 4.2 million households spend more than 5% of income on broadband exceeding ITU affordability benchmark.

cards.big: 4.2 million households spend over 5% of income on broadband access
cards.sub: B40 families pay proportionally 4 times more for internet than T20

cards.big: Average fixed broadband cost of RM120 equals 8% of B40 median income
cards.sub: Price dropped 20% since 2022 but remains unaffordable for bottom 40%

cards.big: Malaysia ranks 7th in ASEAN for broadband affordability behind Vietnam
cards.sub: Both neighbours offer comparable speeds at 40-60% lower monthly prices

cards.big: Government RM50 broadband subsidy reaches only 800,000 of 4.2 million eligible
cards.sub: Application process requires MyKad verification excluding undocumented communities

cards.big: Broadband costs test whether digital access is priced for inclusion or for profit
4.2 million households face a broadband affordability gap. Connectivity is infrastructure, not a consumer luxury.
